candidates.evidence_quote: Company comparable restaurant sales increased 7.5% in the second quarter of fiscal 2026, including 8.6% for Chili’s.
Chili’s delivered another positive quarter in our turnaround with +31% same store sales driven by +21% traffic
Comparable restaurant sales increased 28.2%, with an increase in comparable restaurant sales of 31.6% for Chili’s and 0.4% for Maggiano’s.
Chili’s continues to deliver industry leading results with first quarter sales of +21% and traffic of +13%, against a tough macro environment
The performance at Chili’s exceeded our plans, while Maggiano’s experienced traffic declines.
Guest traffic improved sequentially in the second quarter despite the headwind created by our decision to de-emphasize virtual brands.
Chili’s Company sales increased primarily due to favorable comparable restaurant sales driven by menu pricing, higher traffic, and favorable sales mix.
favorable comparable restaurant sales driven by higher traffic, favorable menu item mix, and menu pricing
partially offset by higher hourly labor, manager salaries and bonus, unfavorable menu item mix, repairs and maintenance, advertising, and workers' compensation and general liability insurance
higher hourly labor and manager salaries, advertising, repairs and maintenance, and other restaurant expense
in the prepared comments, I talked about the wage rate inflation and the commodity inflation
partially offset by higher hourly labor, repairs and maintenance, higher manager salaries and bonus, and unfavorable commodity costs
Our results for the first quarter of fiscal 2024 were primarily driven by an increase in Company sales and favorable food and beverage costs as a percentage of Company sales.
partially offset by increased advertising, hourly labor, and other restaurant expenses
increasing restaurant staffing and repairs and maintenance expense
Chili’s Company restaurant expenses, as a percentage of Company sales, decreased primarily due to sales leverage
a rise in restaurant operating margin (non-GAAP) to 17.8% for the fourth quarter
an increase in operating income margin to 9.8%

increased primarily due to higher incentive compensation and recent technology initiatives

Chili’s sales growth this quarter was driven primarily by continued increases in traffic, supported by menu innovation and advertising that highlights our industry-leading value and encourages guest trial.
The launch of the “Big Smasher” burger and the strength of Chili’s advertising highlighting value drove traffic during the fourth quarter.
The “Big Smasher” burger and Triple Dipper® are resonating well with guests, and our “3 for Me” combos provide a compelling everyday value.
as far as the three for me, we've done a really great job of keeping it very consistent. So it's been in that 18% to 19% range
The ribs upgrade has been a success, with the ribs business now running 35% up in sales and significantly improved profitability
the frozen Patron margarita platform is now selling two times the units of the old platform, despite a higher price point for the more premium ingredients
We are now selling 20% more Southwestern queso and the original skillet queso versus the prior two queso lineup.
our relaunched nachos featuring our signature chicken, bacon, and house-made ranch is now 170% bigger business than the previous nachos
our super premium chicken sandwich lineup, which will launch chain wide in April with a substantial advertising campaign
Chili’s advertising and social media campaigns continued to drive new guests and increased frequency in the first quarter.
Chili’s turnaround, led by guest experience improvements, is sustaining over the long-term
We continue to prioritize our guest experience by enhancing restaurant staffing and focusing on keeping our restaurants well maintained.
we continued reducing our menu and pantry SKUs
our first four remodel pilot restaurants should be completed by the end of this quarter, and we will start getting a read on how they are performing

notes: Reviewed all 55 events in order; 18 events were items-only 8-K/8-K-A boilerplate (officer or compensation changes, shareholder votes, bylaw amendments, debt-agreement covers, exhibit-only filings) with no driver-usable text.

Skipped as vague, non-causal, or instance-bound (14): revolver amendment / senior notes financing detail, LA wildfires mention, analyst stimulus-rebate speculation, loyalty program, tokenized customer data initiative, share-count guidance, dine-in traffic (covered by restaurant_traffic), third-party delivery vs to-go split, regional pricing tiers, north-of-six initiative, Back to Maggiano's strategy label, generic weather (covered by winter_storm), banquet room renovations, bare 'macroeconomic conditions'.

fiscal.ai KPI labels were rewritten to standard driver names; 3 KPI labels (Chili's Company-Owned, Maggiano's Company-Owned, Total Company-Owned comparable restaurant sales) dedup into the event-evidenced chilis_same_store_sales, maggianos_same_store_sales, and same_store_sales.
